title: श्री विश्वकर्मा आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विश्वकर्मा आरती ॥

जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु, जय श्री विश्वकर्मा।
सकल सृष्टि के रचैया, परम पिता ब्रह्मा॥

पञ्चमुखी छवि तुम्हारी, अति मनोहर न्यारी।
सद्योजात वामदेव, अघोर मुख उजियारी॥
जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु...॥

तत्पुरुष ईशान मुखा, पञ्चमुख विराजे।
दिव्य तेज से सुशोभित, त्रिलोक में साजे॥
जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु...॥

सुदर्शन चक्र रचाया, विष्णु को दे आया।
त्रिशूल डमरू शंकर को, वज्र इन्द्र को दे आया॥
जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु...॥

स्वर्ग लोक की रचना की, लंका सोने की बनाई।
द्वारिका पुरी सजाई, इन्द्रपुरी की शोभा बढ़ाई॥
जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु...॥

शिल्पकारों के ईश्वर, यन्त्रों के आधार।
कारीगरों के देवता, करो सबका उद्धार॥
जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु...॥

औज़ारों की पूजा करते, शिल्पी सब मिल आते।
तेरी कृपा से बाबा, सब कारज बन जाते॥
जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु...॥

विश्वकर्मा की आरती, जो कोई नर गावे।
शिल्प कर्म में सिद्धि पावे, सब सुख को पावे॥
जय श्री विश्वकर्मा प्रभु...॥

॥ इति श्री विश्वकर्मा आरती सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishwakarma Aarti ||

Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu, Jai Shri Vishwakarma |
Sakal srishti ke rachaiya, param pita Brahma ||

Panchmukhi chhavi tumhari, ati manohar nyari |
Sadyojat Vamadev, Aghor mukh ujiyari ||
Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu... ||

Tatpurush Ishan mukha, panchmukh viraje |
Divya tej se sushobhit, trilok mein saje ||
Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu... ||

Sudarshan chakra rachaya, Vishnu ko de aaya |
Trishul damru Shankar ko, vajra Indra ko de aaya ||
Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu... ||

Swarg lok ki rachna ki, Lanka sone ki banai |
Dwarika puri sajai, Indrapuri ki shobha badhai ||
Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu... ||

Shilpkaron ke Ishwar, yantron ke adhar |
Karigaron ke devata, karo sabka uddhar ||
Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu... ||

Auzaron ki puja karte, shilpi sab mil aate |
Teri kripa se Baba, sab karaj ban jaate ||
Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu... ||

Vishwakarma ki aarti, jo koi nar gave |
Shilpa karma mein siddhi pave, sab sukh ko pave ||
Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu... ||

|| Iti Shri Vishwakarma Aarti Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: इस आरती में विश्वकर्मा भगवान की स्तुति की गई है। वे पञ्चमुखी (सद्योजात, वामदेव, अघोर, तत्पुरुष, ईशान) हैं और उनका दिव्य तेज त्रिलोक में व्याप्त है। उन्होंने विष्णु के लिए सुदर्शन चक्र, शंकर के लिए त्रिशूल-डमरू और इन्द्र के लिए वज्र का निर्माण किया। स्वर्ग लोक, सोने की लंका, द्वारका और इन्द्रपुरी उनकी रचनाएँ हैं। वे सभी शिल्पकारों और कारीगरों के आराध्य देव हैं।
benefits: Invokes blessings of Lord Vishwakarma for all artisans
Traditionally associated with success in craftsmanship and creative endeavors
Protects tools and machinery from malfunction
Invokes prosperity to workers and their families
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles in professional and business ventures
Especially beneficial on Vishwakarma Jayanti day
description: Vishwakarma Aarti (श्री विश्वकर्मा आरती) is a traditional worship song (aarti) dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Traditional Vishwakarma Bhakti Literature. Regular recitation is believed to grant prosperity to workers and their families, protect tools and machinery from malfunction, and bring success in craftsmanship and creative endeavors. For best results, devotees chant this on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Vishwakarma Jayanti. With 7 verses and a reading time of about 6 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Vishwakarma Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishwakarma Aarti in Hindi with transliteration and meaning. Sacred aarti to divine architect Lord Vishwakarma for craftsmen and workers.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Vishwakarma?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Vishwakarma is the divine architect and celestial engineer of Hindu tradition, described in the Rig Veda, the Mahabharata, and the Puranas as the creator of the cities of the gods (Indraprastha, Lanka, Dwarka), the chariots of the devas, the weapons of Vishnu and Shiva, and the divine thrones and palaces of Vaikuntha and Kailasha. He is regarded as the patron deity of all artisans, engineers, architects, smiths, weavers, carpenters, masons, and craftspersons across the Indian subcontinent. (Source: ServDharm; The Daily Jagran.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Vishwakarma Aarti sung?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishwakarma Aarti is sung most prominently on Vishwakarma Jayanti, observed on Kanya Sankranti (the day the Sun enters Virgo, falling on 16 or 17 September every year) in eastern and northern India, and on Rishi Panchami or the day after Diwali in some regional traditions. The aarti is recited in factories, workshops, garages, construction sites, ironworks, and offices where machinery and tools are first ritually cleaned, then garlanded, anointed with sandalwood, and offered worship. (Source: 99Pandit; Pandit.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Vishwakarma Aarti praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The aarti opens with 'Jai Shri Vishwakarma Prabhu, Jai Shri Vishwakarma, Sakal Srashti Ke Karta, Rakshak Stuti Dharma,' praising him as the maker of all creation and protector of dharma. Subsequent verses describe his role in giving the Vedas to Brahma, in advancing knowledge among living beings, in healing afflicted kings, and in manifesting in many divine forms (one-faced, four-faced, five-faced, three-armed, four-armed, ten-armed). The closing verse promises happiness and prosperity to those who sing it. (Source: ServDharm; The Daily Jagran.)

seoFaqs.question: Who performs the Vishwakarma Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditionally, the puja is performed by the Vishwakarma vansh communities, including Lohar (smiths), Sutar/Badhai (carpenters), Sonar (goldsmiths), Kasera (coppersmiths), Tamrakar (brass-workers), and Shilpkar (sculptors), who trace their lineage to the celestial Vishwakarma. In modern industrial India, the puja has expanded to all workshop owners, factory employees, mechanics, drivers, IT engineers, and software professionals who venerate their tools, machines, vehicles, and computers as instruments of livelihood blessed by Vishwakarma. (Source: Bharat Temples; Pandit.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What rituals accompany the Vishwakarma Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: On Vishwakarma Puja day, all tools, machines, vehicles, and instruments of trade are ritually cleaned, decorated with marigold garlands, anointed with sandalwood paste and turmeric, and placed before an image or photograph of Vishwakarma. The puja includes invocation, offering of sweets (laddu, peda), incense, lamps, and prasad, followed by the singing of the aarti with a five-wick lamp circled clockwise. After the aarti, prasad is distributed to all workers, and machinery is not used for the rest of the day. (Source: 99Pandit; The Daily Jagran.)
